title: streblosa bracteata
intro: Streblosa bracteata, also known as the Borneo hill forest tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The bark of Streblosa bracteata is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark is often dried and made into tea or infused in hot water to reap its medicinal properties.
